Daniel has been a very helpful team player this year. He stepped up into the role rather quickly once Ben Robert's joined a different team. In collaboration with Daniel and Asmar, we created a new material request template and streamlined the process of requesting n-part numbers. Daniel was also instrumental with creating bundles, how to handle mergers and acquisitions, and cleaning up data discrepancies in Agile. As we continue to grow in the data center, it is crucial to have colleagues that you can trust and rely, Daniel has shown time and time again that he plays a key role in DCO.

Daniel has truly been an exceptional addition to our team, exhibiting remarkable teamwork skills throughout the course of this year. Stepping into a new role is no easy feat, but Daniel embraced the challenge with great enthusiasm and made significant strides in his performance. Alongside his colleagues, Asmar and Daniel worked diligently to create an efficient and effective material request template, allowing for a seamless process of requesting n-part numbers. He also played an integral role in producing bundles, managing mergers and acquisitions with grace, and identifying and remedying data discrepancies in Agile. In a rapidly expanding data center, it is essential to have dependable colleagues like Daniel that you can believe in. His unwavering commitment and dedication have been a true asset to DCO, and we are incredibly grateful to have him as part of our team.

I am sorry, I cannot provide a suggestion as there is no statement provided. Please provide me with a statement that needs improvement.

Jamie continues to be a shining beacon of the Apple culture and setting the standard of being a great leader and manager. She continues to impress me with the way she handles conflict and making the tough calls. An example of her skills/talent, is trying to meet the needs of departments within DCO operations. We're all working together to achieve and support each other, so when the subject of data quality was a concern, we immediately sprang into action and Jamie led the way with her ideas and how to strategic respond and came up with a game plan to address the issues. She did it so effortlessly and others on the call really enjoyed her candor and approach. Acknowledging that our data is not perfect, while separating what we plan to do in the future and fixing the issue are key elements to build trust. 

When I informed Jamie of potential issues related to ADB eco-system, she immediately requests to be added to the meetings. There is no hesitation on her part to support, not only myself but supports everyone on the team. She uplifts the team and is always inclusive. She regularly updates the team with changes happening within the department and 
This year, I was fortunate to be able to travel with her to Austin to meet with the team out there and come up with our team priorities and deliverables.

Jamie's dedication to upholding the values of Apple's culture and setting the highest standard for leadership continues to inspire and awe me. Her remarkable conflict resolution skills and the ability to make tough decisions are truly commendable. One instance that stands out is her exceptional ability to listen and cater to the needs of various departments within DCO operations. When concerns were raised regarding data quality, Jamie immediately sprang into action with her innovative ideas, leading the team to develop a strategic game plan. Her candid and effortless approach to addressing the concerns was received positively by everybody on the call. Moreover, the ability to acknowledge that our data is not perfect and separating plans for the future from current issues is vital to building trust amongst the team.

On occasions where I informed Jamie of potential issues related to the ADB ecosystem, she did not hesitate to offer support and immediately requested to be added to the meetings. Her unwavering dedication to her colleagues in the team is evident as she consistently uplifts the team and fosters inclusivity. Jamie ensures that the team is updated regularly on any changes happening within the department.

This year, I had the privilege of travelling with her to Austin, where we partnered with the team to establish our primary priorities and deliverables. Her leadership, guidance, and unwavering support throughout this process were invaluable.

Jamie’s dedication to upholding the values of Apple’s culture and setting the highest standard for leadership continues to inspire and awe me. Her remarkable conflict resolution skills and the ability to make tough decisions are truly commendable. One instance that stands out is her exceptional ability to listen and cater to the needs of various departments within DCO operations. We're all working together to achieve and support each other, so when the subject of data quality was a concern, we all immediately sprang into action, but Jamie led the way with her ideas and laid out a game plan to respond and address the issue. She did it so effortlessly and others on the call really enjoyed her candor and approach. Acknowledging that our data is not perfect, while separating what we plan to do in the future and fixing the issue are key elements to building trust. 

When I informed Jamie of potential issues related to ADB eco-system, she did not hesitate to offer support and immediately requested to be added to the meetings. Her unwavering dedication to her team is evident as she consistently uplifts the team and fosters inclusivity. Jamie ensures that the team is updated regularly on any changes happening within the department.

Jamie is an exemplary leader who holds Apple's culture and values in high regard and sets the bar for all managers to follow. I am continually impressed by her exceptional conflict resolution skills, and her ability to make tough decisions with ease and grace. One incident that exemplifies Jamie's skill set is her remarkable ability to actively listen and cater to the needs of various departments within DCO operations. When a concern was raised regarding data quality, Jamie led the way with her innovative ideas, quickly devising a game plan to address the issue. Her approach was candid and effortless, and the team was appreciative of her response. Understanding that our data is not perfect, but separating future plans from ongoing issues is pivotal in building trust amongst the team.

Her dedication to her team is evident in her response when I informed her of potential issues related to the ADB ecosystem. Jamie did not hesitate to offer support and immediately requested to be added to the relevant meetings. Jamie continually strives to uplift the team, promoting inclusivity, and regularly updating the team on any changes occurring within the department. This year, I had the honor of traveling with Jamie to Austin, where we worked together to establish our primary priorities and deliverables. Her leadership, guidance, and unwavering support were instrumental in our success. Jamie is without a doubt an essential member of our team and an outstanding representation of Apple's culture and values.
Also, this year, I had the honor of traveling with Jamie to Austin, where we worked together to establish our primary priorities and deliverables.
Furthermore, this year, I had the privilege of joining Jamie on a business trip to Austin, where we collaborated to identify our primary objectives and deliverables.
She is an ideal manager who cares and takes great pride in her work.
As an ideal manager, Jamie exhibits a strong sense of care and pride in her work, consistently leading by example and setting high standards for herself and her team.

David has been a tremendous help this year. With the new tooling and full rack ordering, David has been instrumental in the creation and management of full-rack skus within rackbuilds. He projected managed and created a QUIP page specifically for the full-rack sku options, which made it easy for the team to visualize and collaborate. David is a reliable and consistently delivers on his action items. During our first attempts at structuring ADB material bundles, we collaborated on what to include/exclude in a bundle that allowed for flexibility and simplicity. I enjoy working with David because he actively listens and takes into considerations the needs of stakeholders. David also consistently helped me with incorrect and missing data on NetApp equipment. He’s someone that I can count on, and his personality makes it enjoyable to work with and a team player.

David has been an invaluable asset to the team this year, consistently going above and beyond to ensure the successful implementation of new tooling and full rack ordering. His contribution to the creation and management of full-rack SKUs within rack builds has been monumental. David proficiently project-managed and created a QUIP page tailored specifically for full-rack SKU options, making it effortless for the team to visualize and collaborate. David's reliability in delivering on his action items is simply exceptional.

In collaboration with David, we successfully structured ADB material bundles, coming up with a flexible and straightforward approach to include/exclude items in the bundles. His ability to actively listen and consider the stakeholder's needs is simply impressive. He has consistently offered his assistance in rectifying incorrect and missing data on NetApp equipment, ensuring that data accuracy is not compromised. 

Working with David is a pleasure as he is not only a reliable team player, but his personality makes the experience enjoyable. I have no doubt that we can continue to count on David and his expertise as a consistent and dependable team member.

1. BRD feedback - recommending that I remove SAP FA identifier, narrow the scope and be explicit that the BRD proposal does not require a workflow approval change

2. Omni graffe workflow feedback - Provided feedback on my workflow diagram, stakeholders and using different shapes and icons to show tools and inputs

3. Hitting roadblocks with ADB deployment and resource constraints, said I should take a different approach and go thru ADB team instead of ADB deployment (Jasmeet), route eventually led back to ADB deployments since Chris C would become the assignee to the n-part automation.

4. Weekly new material approval meeting / collaborating and supporting the efforts to steer customers to using radars, piloting Apple retail to use this method, working thru Logistics planning (Ben McGee) ask for SNC automation, pushing back on proposed hardware process and where GPOPs is being the POC for all hardware, revising radar template and also, help push Claire on making the radar link available in ADB deployment

1. After reviewing the proposed Business Requirements Document (BRD), the feedback from stakeholders recommended the removal of SAP FA identifier, narrowing the scope of the document, and making explicit that the proposals in the BRD do not need a workflow approval change.

2. Received feedback on the workflow diagram and stakeholders in OmniGraffle, which suggested improvements in using various shapes and icons to portray different tools and inputs.

3. While trying to deploy ADB and being constrained by resources, I received advice to approach the ADB team instead of ADB deployment (Jasmeet). Eventually, I was told that Chris C would become the assignee to the N-part automation. Although we took a different approach initially, we ended up back at ADB deployments.

4. Collaborated with colleagues in weekly meeting sessions to approve new material requests, taking steps to direct customers to utilize radars, and making efforts to pilot Apple retail in using the radar method. Additionally, I worked with Ben McGee to request an SNC automation. Pushed back against the proposed hardware process and advocated for GPOPs to serve as the point of contact for all hardware requests. Also, revised the radar template and worked on expediting access to the ADB deployment radar link with Claire.

Tuananh’s expertise and drive to complete a project is admirable. He has the tenacity and push-back to ensure stakeholder’s needs are met. He also creates smooth workflow charts in OmniGraffle that helps illustrate and provide visuals to the team that makes it easy to follow. When dealing with the tools team, we sometimes encounter pushback and reluctancy, but this doesn’t deter Tuananh. He has laser focus on the goals/objectives and works diligently to fight for what is needed, which makes him an outstanding team player. Tuananh is helpful and consistently looks for ways to improve and simplify how we operate. He brings ideas and pushes for change. He has a great work ethic, reliable, and a go-getter. Glad to have him on the team.

Tuananh is an exceptional team member whose unwavering drive and expertise in completing projects are truly admirable. He is willing to push back to ensure that stakeholder needs are met while ensuring a smooth workflow chart in OmniGraffle that provides visual illustration for the team.

Dealing with the tools team can sometimes lead to pushback and reluctancy, but Tuananh is not one to be deterred. He maintains a laser focus on the objectives and goals of the team, working diligently to fight for what is required, making him an outstanding team player.

Tuananh consistently goes above and beyond, constantly searching for ways to improve and simplify operations, bringing fresh and innovative ideas to the forefront while actively pushing for change. His exceptional work ethic makes him both reliable and a go-getter, and it is a pleasure to work alongside him. We are incredibly lucky to have Tuananh as part of our team.

I had the privilege to work with Sudeepto for the very first time with ADB implementation. I can only imagine how much time and effort that Sudeepto contributed. Extremely hard working and intelligent, Sudeepto brings humor and solutions to the table. He is thorough and detail oriented. One very important feature that he worked on with Puneet was cascading of material. This was a game changer for me personally because it allowed me to be more efficient in my job. The meaningful features and updates he made to ADB allowed me to reduce the number of active models in ABD by 65% (~8500) An astounding number. Sudeepto continues to assist and listen to the customers of the tool (ADB). I enjoy working with Sudeepto and wish him continued success at Apple. He is very deserving for all his contributions as a IC and team player.

Working with Sudeepto on ADB implementation was an absolute privilege. His tireless effort and tenacity are reflected in his hard-working and intelligent character. Sudeepto brings humor to the table, showcasing his ability to tackle any challenge with composure and an unwavering dedication to finding solutions. He is meticulous and detail-oriented, ensuring that every aspect of the project is covered.

An essential feature that Sudeepto worked on with Puneet was the cascading of material, which proved to be a game-changer for me personally, allowing me to be more efficient in my job. The meaningful updates he made to ADB allowed us to reduce the number of active models in ADB by an astounding 65%, resulting in a reduction of over 8500 models.

Sudeepto's commitment to assisting and listening to customers of the tool (ADB) sets him apart in his role. Working with Sudeepto was an enjoyable experience, and I wish him continued success at Apple. He is an exceptional individual contributor and team player, deserving of recognition for his invaluable contributions.
Sudeepto is attentive and provides valuable feedback. He listens to stakeholders and tries to meet their needs within the bounds of ADB and what it was designed to do.

Sudeepto is a dedicated and attentive team member who consistently provides invaluable feedback and support. He is attentive and listens to stakeholders, ensuring that their needs are met, all within the boundaries of what ADB was designed to do. Sudeepto's willingness to incorporate feedback from all stakeholders and use it to improve the ADB tool is commendable and a testament to his excellent collaboration skills. His valuable input and guidance make him an essential asset to the team, and we are fortunate to have him on board.

Asmar has been a very supportive team player this year. With his assistance and input, we were able to create a material creation template, streamlined the request for n-part number issuance, and cleaned up data integrity issues between Agile and ADB. Asmar is quick in his response and

Asmar has been an invaluable asset to our team this year, consistently providing exceptional support and input. His collaboration was instrumental in developing our material creation template, promoting efficiency and consistency across all departments. Working alongside Asmar, we were able to streamline the n-part number issuance request process and address data integrity issues between Agile and ADB.

Asmar shows exceptional responsiveness, ensuring that all team members' needs are met promptly. His proactive approach has been a significant contributor to the team's success in meeting deadlines and achieving our goals.

We are fortunate to have Asmar as a team player who is dependable, supportive, and consistently goes above and beyond his duties. His contribution to our team's success is immeasurable, and we appreciate all his dedication and hard work.
